chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 348 of 2024 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on MA No.2583/2024 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 3. For orders on MA No.2584/2024 (Ex./A) 4. For hearing of bail application 27.05.2024 Mr. Waseem Khan, Advocate for applicants / accused..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgency is granted. 2. Deferred. 3. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 4. Through this bail application, applicants / accused Salman Ali S/o Mushtaque Hussain and Anees ur Rehman S/o Habib ur Rehman seek protective bail in Crime No.39 of 2024, registered at Police Station Sarhad, District Ghotki u/s 489-F, 506/2, 34 PPC. Learned Counsel contends that applicants are innocent, have been falsely implicated in this case. He further contends that applicants are ready to appear before the Court concerned along with an application u/s 498 CrPC to prove their innocence. However, they apprehend their immediate arrest as non-bailable warrants have been issued against them. Hence, they seek protective bail. Without touching merits of the case, protective bail for a period of six days w.e.f. 27.05.2024 to 01.06.2024 (both days inclusive) is granted to applicants subject to their furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.10,000/- (Rupees ten thousand) each and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. Operation of non-bailable warrants in this case, if any, shall remain suspended during aforesaid period, in which applicants are directed to surrender before the Court concerned.

Without touching merits of the case, protective bail for a period of six days w.e.f. 27.05.2024 to 01.06.2024 (both days inclusive) is granted to applicants subject to their furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.10,000/- (Rupees ten thousand) each and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. Operation of non-bailable warrants in this case, if any, shall remain suspended during aforesaid period, in which applicants are directed to surrender before the Court concerned. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of above mentioned period or whenever applicants surrender before the Court concerned, which one occurs earlier. It is, however, made clear that in case applicants fail to surrender before the Court concerned within stipulated period, their bail bonds shall stand automatically forfeited. The bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Abdul Basit
